name: Ruqayya bint Hasan
title: Wife of the Ambassador
shortDescription: Daughter of Imam Hasan Mujtaba (AS) and wife of Muslim ibn Aqeel who mourned her husband and sons in Karbala.
fullStory:
Ruqayya bint Hasan ibn Ali (AS) was Imam Hasan Mujtaba's (AS) daughter who married her father's cousin, Muslim ibn Aqeel. (Muslim also had another wife named Ruqayya bint Ali).

Ruqayya was present in Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan. Before reaching Karbala, she heard the painful news of her loyal husband's (Muslim ibn Aqeel) martyrdom in Kufa and put on mourning clothes.

On Ashura, she was not only mourning her husband but also witnessed the martyrdom of her sons (Abdullah ibn Muslim and Muhammad ibn Muslim) while defending their uncle Imam Hussain (AS). She also lost her brothers (Qasim, Abu Bakr, and Abdullah ibn Hasan).

Ruqayya bint Hasan was a symbol of resistance and a mountain of pain in the captives' caravan. She was the wife of a martyr, mother of martyrs, and sister of martyrs who endured the sufferings of the route to Kufa and Sham with firmness, keeping the message of Ashura alive.
quote: (Her condition in mourning Muslim:) O Muslim! You preceded us to Paradise, and we shall join you...
